rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2021 line 1. RESPONDENTS JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA and VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA's Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award is continued to April 23, 2021. By no later than March 12, 2021 petitioner to file a motion to confirm/vacate arbitration award and set it for hearing on April 23, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Petitioners First Second Third And Fourth Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2021 line 1. RESPONDENTS JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA and VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA's Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Petitioner's First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action is DENIED. No statutory or other basis is stated for the relief requested. This motion does not appear to be a motion for judgment on the pleadings as it is not accompanied by a meet and confer declaration. This motion does not appear to be a motion for summary judgment as it does not provide the requisite notice and is not accompanied by the necessary filings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAURA FRANCES AMBROSENO ET AL VS. DIOSDADO BANATAO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS DIOSDADO BANATAO and MARIA BANATAO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. No authority on point is cited and no evidence is provided that the term "otherwise" as used in the easement clause does not contemplate a temporary obstruction created by objects/people being temporarily on the roof. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: A STAR HOLDINGS LLC VS. JUSTIN MEADOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF A STAR HOLDINGS LLC's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Complete verified responses without objections to special interrogatories and requests for production to be provided within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $960. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
